articles.headline: Over Half of U.S. Data Center Projects Delayed or Canceled, Boosting Value of Existing AI Infrastructure
articles.summary: More than half of planned U.S. data center builds are delayed or canceled due to supply shortages, power constraints, and reliance on Chinese imports, elevating the value of existing AI facilities amid surging demand. Tech giants like Amazon and Alphabet continue massive investments, but bottlenecks shift competitive advantage to operators with ready capacity. This underscores supply chain fragility, energy risks, and infrastructure chokepoints for businesses planning AI expansions.

articles.headline: Broadcom Announces Extended Partnership with Meta to Support Multi-Gigawatts of Custom Silicon
articles.summary: Broadcom extended its technology partnership with Meta to deploy solutions for multi-gigawatt-scale custom AI silicon, deepening hyperscaler demand for AI compute. This enhances revenue visibility for chip suppliers and highlights secular growth in AI infrastructure spending. Enterprises assessing hardware partnerships note strengthened supply chain ties amid capacity races.

articles.headline: ZTE Highlights Path to Broadband Monetization at FTTH Conference 2026
articles.summary: ZTE outlined strategies for broadband providers to monetize networks amid rising data demands from AI and cloud services. This offers SMEs and enterprises tools to enhance connectivity revenue streams and reduce infrastructure costs. Operational efficiency gains are possible for businesses upgrading digital tools.
